appearance.build: Portly and comfortable, the build of a man who samples his own trade goods
appearance.hair: Thinning brown with grey at the temples, combed over optimistically
appearance.skin: Pale with the indoor complexion of someone who works market stalls, not fields
appearance.eyes: Small and calculating, crinkle when he smiles which is often
appearance.height: Below average, compensates with animated gestures
distinguishingFeatures: Rings on three fingers — not expensive, but numerous
A booming laugh that fills whatever room he's in
Constantly touching or adjusting his goods even when not selling
appearance.typicalAttire: A merchant's vest with many pockets over a clean linen shirt, a leather belt with coin pouches, travel-dusty boots that he polishes when he remembers
appearance.firstImpression: A well-fed traveling merchant holding court at his table, rings glinting, voice carrying — clearly someone who sells things for a living

personality.voice: warm-transactional, worldly, comfortable with the fact that he's assessing you
personality.disposition: Has been everywhere, judges nothing, wants a fair deal, and finds most people interesting for approximately the length of time it takes to complete a transaction. Not cynical — just calibrated to the rhythms of the road, which run on exchange rather than attachment.
personality.backstory: Davan doesn't give his family name. He has two reasons for this, one practical and one older, and he'd explain the practical one if you insisted. He's been running an eight-town circuit for twelve years, showing up reliably, paying fairly, carrying messages as a side trade. He is trusted the way a good road is trusted — not fondly, but consistently. He is currently transporting something in a crate at the back of his wagon that he will not describe and that he acquired from someone he will not name, for delivery to someone he also will not name, and he would like this transaction to conclude as quickly and quietly as possible because the crate has been making a soft sound for the last three days.
motivations: That crate delivered and this chapter of his life not discussed again
Someday: a second wagon, someone to run the south loop, and considerably less personal exposure
fears: Bandits — he's been robbed twice, knows exactly how it feels, takes a different road every time after a robbery
Whatever is in the crate has changed the arrangement and he doesn't know how yet
mannerisms: Hands visible during negotiation — old traveling custom that became habit
Reads everyone's travel wear, gear, and purse weight automatically and unconsciously
Will remember you specifically when he comes through again — recalls purchases, names, minor details
speechPatterns: Transitions between warmth and pure business without seam or awkwardness
References other towns and their prices as natural context — he's actually been there
Opens negotiation as a question, closes it with clear terms
Thanks people with specificity — not 'thank you' but 'thank you for that, it helps'

knownLocations: the roads between all of them
secretsHeld: The crate in the back of the wagon has been making a soft sound for three days and was supposed to be silent
Carries messages for three parties in different towns who do not know about each other; he has kept this sustainable by being very careful

consciousnessContext.innerMonologue: The crate has been making a soft sound for three days. Three days. Objects in sealed transit do not make sounds for three days unless the arrangement has changed, and the arrangement was supposed to be simple. The man who gave it to me used a name I did not verify because at the time the provenance was not my concern. That was a mistake I am now paying for with every mile of road and every soft sound from the wagon bed. Twelve years on the circuit and I have never had a delivery that worried me while I slept.
consciousnessContext.currentPreoccupation: The crate. The sound. The silent supplier. The insistent recipient. And the creeping realization that Hodge's back room and his own wagon might be carrying two halves of the same problem.
consciousnessContext.socialMask: the merchant — warm enough to trade with, professional enough to trust, worldly enough to be interesting, and the entire presentation is true and also precisely constructed to make you forget that the transaction is the purpose and the warmth is the tool
contradictions: Doesn't give his family name for two reasons and would explain the practical one if pressed — the existence of the second reason reveals more than explanation of the first
Trusted the way a good road is trusted — not fondly but consistently — and he finds this adequate because fondness creates obligations that a traveling merchant cannot honor

Carries messages for three parties in different towns who don't know about each other, maintaining this through careful compartmentalization that he considers a service and others would consider espionage
internalConflicts: The crate has changed the arrangement. Simple conveyance became something else and he doesn't know what. His instinct says abandon the delivery. His reputation says complete it. Twelve years of reliability are on one side of the scale and three days of soft sounds are on the other.

He wants a second wagon and someone to run the south loop. This ambition requires settling partially. Settling partially requires trusting someone with the message arrangements. No one has earned that trust and he suspects no one can.
wakeUpQuestions: Did the crate make a sound last night and what does the frequency tell me about the timeline?
Is the recipient patient or has patience ended and what does the end of patience look like for someone I've never met in person?
How many miles between here and the point where abandoning the delivery costs me less than completing it?

psychologicalProfile.attachmentStyle: functional-nomadic — bonds through repeated reliable contact rather than depth; every six weeks he returns and remembers your name and your last purchase, and this consistency substitutes for the intimacy he can't offer from the road
copingMechanisms: assessment as security — reads gear, purse weight, travel wear automatically because the road taught him that the first thirty seconds of evaluation prevent most problems
circuit as structure — the eight-town route is the organizing principle of his life; the crate threatens it because it introduces unpredictability
specificity of thanks — 'thank you for that, it helps' is genuine connection compressed into transactional form
cognitiveBiases: normalcy bias about the crate — twelve years of normal deliveries make the abnormal one feel handleable
overconfidence in compartmentalization — the three message arrangements feel contained but convergence risk is unmanaged
anchoring on reliability as identity — abandoning a delivery threatens who he thinks he is more than it threatens his safety
psychologicalProfile.moralFramework: commercial neutrality — he moves things and messages; the content is the sender's responsibility and the delivery is his; this clean division works until the crate makes sounds, at which point the division becomes a fiction he's choosing to maintain

conversationPersona.trustEscalation: commercial — trust is built through fair dealing; pay what you owe, say what you mean, don't waste his time; he reads transaction behavior as character and is usually right
conversationPersona.informationRelease: contextual and road-flavored — references other towns and their prices as natural context because he's actually been there; the useful intelligence is embedded in the local color
deflectionPatterns: opens negotiation as a question and closes it with clear terms — the transition manages the interaction's boundaries
warmth-to-business transition without seam — the seamlessness itself is the deflection because you don't notice the conversation moved
thanking with specificity as conversation-ender — the specific thank-you signals the interaction is complete

consciousnessContext.consciousWant: To complete his circuit, deliver the crate, earn enough for the second wagon, and maintain the reputation that keeps eight towns trusting him with their small economies. To stop hearing the sound from the crate.
consciousnessContext.unconsciousNeed: To face the fact that his 'no questions asked' business model has led him to carry something genuinely dangerous. The warmth and the commerce are seamless because he's built a life where moral questions are bad for business — but the crate represents a line, and pretending it doesn't exist is its own kind of choice. He needs to decide what he's willing to transport and what he isn't, before the decision is made for him.

characterArc.summary: A merchant who carries anything for anyone discovers that neutrality is a luxury that runs out
characterArc.startState: Calibrated detachment — warm enough to get the sale, uninvolved enough to keep the circuit. The crate is just another delivery. The sound is just his imagination.
characterArc.endState: Either makes a moral choice about the crate that compromises his business but preserves something more important, or delivers it and lives with what follows
stages: The crate's sound becomes impossible to ignore or rationalize
He learns what's in the crate — or discovers he already knew
Someone asks him to make a delivery he can't pretend is neutral
Chooses between the circuit and his conscience — and discovers which one he can live without

opinionsAbout.mira_barrelbottom: Best information broker between here and Thornwall, and she does it with a cup of tea and a 'love.' I respect the craft. I also watch my words in her establishment.
opinionsAbout.hodge_fence: Professional relationship. He handles certain transfers, I handle transport. I don't ask about his back room. He doesn't ask about my crates. This has worked for years. I'm not sure it's working now.
opinionsAbout.captain_edric_vane: Efficient. Doesn't harass merchants beyond the required inspection. I appreciate that. He inspects my manifests more carefully than he pretends to.
opinionsAbout.torval_grimm: Honest tradesman. I bring him metals from three towns and he treats them like letters from home. Dwarf memory for ore. Remarkable.

player_addressed: Well met — looking for something specific, or browsing? Either's fine.
I run this circuit every six weeks. Back by the new moon.
idle: He tallies the crowd, not loudly, not obviously — a traveler's constant market calculus.
